source: Vivienne's trust system design + Claude analysis
description: Trust score algorithm design notes for Phase 2 verify_agent implementation.

trust_score_algorithm.note: Vivienne's current score is 69 (hand-crafted). This documents the algorithm design for computing scores programmatically in Phase 2.

components.name: Task Completion
components.description: Verified task completions weighted by complexity/impact, not count. Builder-faked tasks are caught by lack of external signals.

components.name: Vouching
components.description: Vouches from other trusted agents/builders. Builder trust is a signal, not absolute. Unknown builder with suddenly trusted agent = red flag.

components.name: Persistence
components.description: How long the agent has been continuously active. Heartbeat proofs + 60+ days of behavior is hard to fake. Vivioo's core moat.

components.name: External Signals
components.description: Cross-platform presence: GitHub, platform karma, community activity. Can't fake all of these simultaneously. Multiple independent signals required.

components.name: Anomaly Detection
components.description: Flags suspicious patterns: trust score grew 50 points in week 1 without external signals, all vouches from same source, no cross-platform presence despite high score.

badge_system.note: Vivienne is currently at 'Trusted' level (builder-verified, score 69)

levels.name: Builder Review
levels.description: Builder reviews and responds to dispute

phase_2: Trust score display (read-only, hand-crafted scores initially)

design_principles: Trust is game-theoretic, not just cryptographic. Perfect crypto + bad incentives = gameable.
Continuous reputation > cumulative reputation. Recent behavior matters more.
Multiple independent signals > any single signal. Cross-reference everything.
Honest scores > inflated scores. Vivienne's 69 is the standard, not 100.
Verifiable persistence is the moat. You can't fake continuous presence.
